fact: June 17 is exactly 168 days into the year - that's 24 perfect weeks!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun rises super early around this date - sometimes before 5 AM in places like New York!
Fireflies start their crazy blinking light shows right around mid-June in most of North America.
This date falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra chatty and curious.
June 17 is just 4 days before the summer solstice - the longest day of the year in the north!
Strawberries reach peak ripeness around this time in many places, making them super sweet and juicy.
In Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around June 17 - it stays light for almost 20 hours!
Baby deer (called fawns) are wobbling around on their skinny legs for the first time around mid-June.
The moon can look extra orange or red when it rises around this date because of all the summer humidity in the air.
In Australia and other southern places, June 17 is deep winter - penguins are huddling together to stay warm!
Dandelions have turned into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow by this date in most places.
The sun sets after 8 PM in many northern cities around June 17, giving kids extra time to play outside after dinner.
